channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Lindor homers as Mets stop Dodgers' scoreless streak in 7-3 win, head to NY with NLCS tied 1-1
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The New York Mets weren't sure what they were going to get with the Los Angeles Dodgers sending out a parade of relievers in a bullpen game. They found out right away.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Texans' Mario Edwards Jr. suspended 4 games without pay for violating NFL's PED policy
item.description: Houston Texans veteran defensive lineman Mario Edwards Jr. has been suspended without pay for the next 4 games for violating the league's performance-enhancing drugs policy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rizzo at first base for Yankees in ALCS opener against Cleveland, 16 days after fracturing fingers
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Anthony Rizzo was back in the New York Yankees' lineup for Monday night's American League Championship Series opener, just 16 days after fracturing a pair of fingers when hit by a pitch.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Eagles coach Nick Sirianni apologizes for his behavior toward fans at the Linc
item.description: Philadelphia Eagles coach Nick Sirianni apologized Monday for gesturing and jawing at fans in the final moments of a win where restless fans booed a listless performance and “Fire Nick!”.
item.dc:creator: Dan Gelston, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: No. 1 Texas' dominant defense will be tested by No. 5 Georgia and senior QB Carson Beck
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — By any measure, from the official statistics to the informal eye test, top-ranked Texas' defense has been dominant. The Longhorns rank No. 1 in total defense and scoring defense. They are top 10 in first downs allowed, tackles for loss and passing yards.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Islanders coach Patrick Roy returns to Colorado and the building where his retired number hangs
item.description: DENVER (AP) — New York Islanders coach Patrick Roy stepped onto the ice at Ball Arena and briefly glanced toward the rafters. Yep, his familiar No. 33 was still up there with all the other banners representing retired Colorado Avalanche numbers.
item.dc:creator: PAT GRAHAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dodgers' Flaherty shares NLCS Game 1 'moment' with mother
item.description: The Los Angeles Dodgers' major trade-deadline acquisition from this summer is already worth it. Jack Flaherty was the star of the show in Game 1 of the NLCS between L.A. and the New York Mets, as he pitched an electric seven shutout innings while giving up just two hits in a 9-0 win.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Will they play or not? Another twist in San Jose State transgender women’s volleyball player controversy
item.description: Nevada officials say their players' decision not to play San Jose State, " does not represent the position of the University. The University intends to move forward with the match as scheduled, and the players may choose not to participate in the match on the day of the contest.
item.dc:creator: Laurence Miedema, The Mercury News

item.title: Brandon Nimmo in Mets' lineup for Game 2 of NLCS despite left foot issue
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Brandon Nimmo was in the New York Mets’ lineup for Game 2 of the National League Championship Series on Monday despite dealing with plantar fasciitis in his left foot. The left fielder was limping throughout the Mets’ 9-0 loss to the Dodgers in Game 1 on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jerry West becomes first three-time inductee as Carter and Billups enter basketball's Hall of Fame
item.description: SPRINGFIELD, Mass. (AP) — The stage was filled with Laker greats when Jerry West made history Sunday night by becoming the first three-time inductee to the Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MAHONEY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Bears vs. Jaguars: Teams battling in early-morning tilt at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium in London
item.description: UPDATE: The Bears and Jaguars have announced their inactives for Sunday's game. Inactives for the Bears are DB Jaquan Brisker, DB Tyrique Stevenson, OL Nate Davis, DL Zacch Pickens, WR Velus Jones Jr., DB Terrell Smith and DL Dominque Robinson.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Los Angeles Dodgers announce starting pitcher for NLCS Game 1
item.description: The Los Angeles Dodgers relied on spectacular pitching to escape the NL Division Series in five games against the San Diego Padres, and it's nearly time to see if those performances will carry over into the NL Championship Series.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Rising returns, but Utes fall flat against ASU, 27-19
item.description: TEMPE, Ariz. (ABC4 Sports) - Cam Rising was back at quarterback, but the Utes offense continued to struggle to score touchdowns in a 27-19 loss at Arizona State Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Yamamoto outduels Darvish in historic matchup as Dodgers beat Padres 2-0 to reach NLCS
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Los Angeles Dodgers shelled out $1 billion for Japanese talent in the offseason and it's paying off in the playoffs.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Kucherov scores go-ahead goal, two empty-netters to push Lightning past Hurricanes in season opener
item.description: Nikita Kucherov had a third-period hat trick to help the Tampa Bay Lightning beat the Carolina Hurricanes 4-1 in Friday night's season opener for both teams.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Fruita Softball wins 13th straight to take home Southwestern League title
item.description: Attention Colorado high school softball teams: you do not want to see the Fruita Monument Wildcats right now.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Utah HC wins overtime thriller over Islanders, 5-4
item.description: LONG ISLAND, N.Y. (ABC4 Sports) - In its first ever road game, the Utah Hockey Club proved to be extremely resilient. Dylan Guenther scored his second goal at 2:18 of overtime, and the Utah Hockey Club rallied for a 5-4 win against the New York Islanders at UBS Arena on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Mets win, advance to NLCS for the first time since 2015
item.description: NEW YORK — Francisco Lindor hit a grand slam in the sixth inning, his latest clutch swing in an extraordinary season full of them, and the New York Mets reached the National League Championship Series with a 4-1 victory over the Philadelphia Phillies on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Ahead of team's game in London, Bears president says he remains steadfast on proceeding with plans for new lakefront stadium
item.description: WARE, England (AP) — The Bears remain focused on the city’s lakefront as the location for a nearly $5 billion stadium development project, team president and CEO Kevin Warren reiterated Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: San Diego Padres urge fans to 'stay classy'
item.description: San Diego Padres management is asking fans to demonstrate good behavior as the contentious National League Division Series shifts from Dodger Stadium to Petco Park on Tuesday, tied at one game apiece.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5
